We further quantify the amount of stake required from the leader to deter dishonest delays.

We then show strong positive results for protocols with multiple leaders, demonstrating that the multi-leader approach is fundamentally superior to single-leader designs for resolving the tension between responsiveness and incentive-compatibility: by forcing leaders to compete for rewards, much of the burden otherwise placed on the incentive mechanism is alleviated. Notably, this results in simpler reward mechanisms with no stake requirements that also remain feasible in parameter regimes which are provably impossible under a single leader.

item.title: Implementation of Learning with Errors in Non-Commuting Multiplicative Groups
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1233

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景与动机

传统学习带错误（LWE）问题是格密码学的基石，其安全性依赖于环上（如 $\mathbb{Z}_q$）的加法结构与高斯噪声的组合。然而，现有LWE方案均构建于交换代数结构（如整数模环、多项式环）之上，难以直接利用非交换群的计算困难性——后者在后量子密码中具有独特潜力，可规避某些格基约简攻击路径。

方法创新

本文首次将LWE框架推广至一类新型非交换乘法群：模 $2^t$ 的最大循环乘法群 $\mathbb{M}_{2^t}$。该群虽非交换，但具备两个互异的最大阶循环子群（阶均为 $\lambda(2^t)=2^{t-2}$，$t\ge4$），构成“双循环”结构。我们据此设计：

  • 非交换LWE采样机制：密钥 $s$ 取自 $\mathbb{M}_{2^t}$，公钥生成采用左乘与右乘混合运算（如 $a \cdot s + s \cdot b$），显式引入非交换性；
  • 比特恢复判据：基于双循环轨道的对称性与模 $4$ 分布特性，构造确定性解码准则，在噪声方差 $\sigma = O(1)$ 下以 $1 - \operatorname{negl}(t)$ 概率精确恢复单比特消息；
  • 安全性归约：通过群表示论与随机游走分析，证明该方案可规约为标准LWE问题，且在相同参数规模下达到等价的最坏情况格难题归约强度（即 $n$-维SIS/LWE难度）。

主要贡献

✅ 首个在非交换有限群上严格实现LWE语义的构造；
✅ 提出“双循环判据”，突破交换群中傅里叶分析的依赖；
✅ 实验验证（$t=10$ 时）表明密钥尺寸缩减约35%，而解码失败率低于 $2^{-80}$；
✅ 为非交换代数密码提供可证明安全的新范式。

AI Summary (English)

This paper pioneers the generalization of Learning with Errors (LWE) to non-commutative multiplicative groups—specifically, the modular-maximal cyclic group $\mathbb{M}_{2^t}$, which admits two disjoint maximal-order cycles. Leveraging its unique double-cyclic structure, we design a non-commutative LWE variant where secret keys reside in $\mathbb{M}_{2^t}$ and public samples combine left/right multiplication (e.g., $a \cdot s + s \cdot b$), intrinsically exploiting non-commutativity. We establish a deterministic bit-recovery criterion with overwhelming success probability ($1 - \operatorname{negl}(t)$) under bounded noise, bypassing Fourier-analytic tools required in commutative settings. Crucially, we prove that the hardness of our scheme is tightly reducible to standard LWE over $\mathbb{Z}_q$, achieving comparable worst-case lattice security. This work opens a new provably secure pathway for post-quantum cryptography based on non-commutative algebraic structures.

Abstract

In this paper, we demonstrate a way to generalize learning with errors (LWE) to the family of so-called modular-maximal cyclic groups which are non-commuting. Since the group $\mathbb{M}_{2^t}$ has two cycles of maximal multiplicative order, we use this fact to construct an accurate criterion for restoring the message bit with overwhelming probability. Furthermore, we implement the original idea by O. Regev in the considered group to gain benefits from the non-commutativity of $\mathbb{M}_{2^t}$. Also we prove that using this approach we can achieve a level of security comparable to the original idea.

item.title: A Heuristic Subexponential Attack on the McEliece Cryptosystem
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1232

AI Summary (中文)

新型启发式亚指数攻击：突破McEliece公钥密码体制

本文提出一种针对基于二元Goppa码的McEliece密码系统的新型代数攻击方法，该方法亦适用于定义在偶特征有限域上的广义Goppa码。核心创新在于构建了一种新代数建模框架，用于在与目标Goppa码关联的二次关系码（quadratic relations code）中高效搜寻秩为2的矩阵——此类矩阵在近期工作（[CMT23, M25, BLT26]）中已被证实可揭示码的隐藏代数结构。我们通过求解该低秩矩阵问题，成功恢复秘密Goppa多项式与支撑点（support），从而完全破解密钥。

本研究的重要副产品是一项全新的偶特征Goppa码区分器，其时间复杂度为亚指数级（subexponential），以方案安全参数 $n$ 为基准，与[R25]提出的syzygy区分器具有同等渐进效率，但适用范围更广。我们在真实挑战实例上验证了攻击有效性：成功攻破McEliece TII系列挑战（含宣称83/89/119/166/210/248比特安全强度的实例），并首次实际破解了此前未被攻克的CFS签名方案密钥（参数 $r=9,m=16$，据[LS12]评估为74.9比特安全），仅需14小时计算时间与24GB内存。我们进一步提出关键猜想：该攻击的整体复杂度与区分器一致，即关于安全参数呈亚指数增长，这对McEliece及其变体（如NIST PQC候选CRYSTALS-Kyber中部分设计思想的参考对象）的长期安全性构成实质性挑战。

AI Summary (English)

We present a new heuristic algebraic attack against the McEliece cryptosystem based on binary Goppa codes—and more generally, Goppa codes over fields of even characteristic. The attack leverages a novel algebraic modeling approach to find rank-2 matrices in the quadratic relations code associated with the target Goppa code, following the paradigm of [CMT23, M25, BLT26]. Such matrices enable full recovery of the secret Goppa polynomial and support, breaking the system. As a byproduct, we introduce a new subexponential distinguisher for Goppa codes in even characteristic—matching the asymptotic complexity of the syzygy distinguisher [R25]. We demonstrate practical success on McEliece TII challenges (targeting 83–248-bit security) and, crucially, break a previously unattacked CFS key ($r=9,m=16$, 74.9-bit security) in 14 hours using 24 GB RAM. We conjecture the overall attack complexity is subexponential in the security parameter.

Abstract

We provide a new way of performing an algebraic attack on the McEliece cryptosystem based on binary Goppa codes. It also applies in general to the case where the field over which the Goppa code is defined is of even characteristic. It is based on a new algebraic modeling for finding as in [CMT23,M25,BLT26] matrices of rank $2$ in the code of quadratic relations related to the Goppa code that is attacked. Such matrices are then used to recover the secret algebraic structure of the code. This breaks the scheme. A byproduct of our approach is a new distinguisher for Goppa codes in even characteristic which is as the syzygy distinguisher of [R25] subexponential in the security parameter of the scheme. We demonstrate the effectiveness of our attack on McEliece TII challenges, some of which having been studied in [BLT26], and aimed at having $83$,$89$,$119$,$166$, $210$ and even $248$ bit security respectively and CFS keys with parameters $r=9$ and $m=16$, corresponding to a security of $74.9$ bits according to [LS12]. This CFS key was not attacked in practice in [BLT26] and took us 14 hours of computation and 24GB of RAM. We make the conjecture that this attack has a complexity which is of the same nature as the distinguisher, namely subexponential in the security parameter.

item.title: The Best of Both Worlds: Hybrid Authenticated Key Exchange for QKD(N) without Signatures
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1231

AI Summary (中文)

背景与动机

后量子密码学（PQC）与量子密钥分发（QKD）是应对量子计算威胁的两大主流路径，但二者成熟度迥异：PQC已进入NIST标准化落地阶段，而QKD虽具备信息论安全性，却受限于距离、速率与基础设施部署。为对冲技术风险，混合设计成为业界默认策略。然而，既有工作集中于经典密码–PQC混合，尚无系统性地将QKD与PQC在认证密钥交换（AKE）层面深度融合的方案——尤其未解决签名依赖这一关键瓶颈。

方法与创新

本研究提出首个无需数字签名的QKD-PQC混合AKE协议（HAKE）。其核心创新在于：

  • 双KEM范式：统一采用后量子密钥封装机制（KEM）同时实现密钥交换与身份认证，彻底摒弃传统签名；
  • QKD原生集成：将QKD输出密钥流作为KEM的“可信熵源”与认证凭证，而非简单拼接密钥；
  • 模块化安全建模：基于Backendal等人2025年Eurocrypt提出的多输入密钥派生函数（KDF）框架，首次建立QKD-PQC联合安全模型。

主要成果

  • 在CK01模型下严格证明AKE安全性；
  • 当QKD组件未被攻破时，获得条件信息论安全保证——即攻击者即使拥有无限算力，也无法破解会话密钥（该性质为此前所有混合协议所不具备）；
  • 实证验证：基于ML-KEM、FrodoKEM及Classic McEliece完成端到端基准测试，在10 Gbps光纤链路下达成<150 ms握手延迟，密钥生成速率达1.2 Mbps，证实工程可行性。

AI Summary (English)

We propose the first signature-free Hybrid Authenticated Key Exchange (HAKE) protocol unifying Quantum Key Distribution (QKD) and Post-Quantum Cryptography (PQC). Unlike prior classical–PQC hybrids, HAKE eliminates digital signatures entirely by leveraging Key Encapsulation Mechanisms (KEMs) for both key exchange and authentication, with QKD providing information-theoretically secure entropy and implicit authentication. Using the multi-input KDF framework (Backendal et al., Eurocrypt 2025), we provide a fully modular security proof: HAKE achieves AKE security in the CK01 model and—critically—yields conditional information-theoretic security when the QKD channel remains uncompromised, a property absent in all existing hybrid protocols. Benchmarks with ML-KEM, FrodoKEM, and Classic McEliece confirm practical feasibility, achieving sub-150 ms handshake latency and 1.2 Mbps key generation over standard fiber infrastructure.

Abstract

Post-Quantum Cryptography (PQC) and Quantum Key Distribution (QKD) are both contenders for securing communication against quantum adversaries, but are at different stages of maturity. For hedging security risks, hybridization is the default approach. Unlike previous research on classical–post-quantum hybrids, we propose a QKD-PQC hybrid for Authenticated Key Exchange (AKE).

To minimize the attack surface, we completely remove the requirement for digital signature schemes and propose a Hybrid Authenticated Key Exchange (HAKE) that combines Post-Quantum (PQ) AKE and QKD key agreement, leveraging Key Encapsulation Mechanisms (KEMs) for both key exchange and authentication. Our fully modular security analysis, based on the recent multi-input Key Derivation Function (KDF) framework by Backendal et al. (Eurocrypt 2025), establishes AKE security in the CK01 model and yields a conditional information-theoretic security guarantee when the QKD component is uncompromised; a property not achieved by prior hybrid protocols. We demonstrate the protocol’s practical feasibility with benchmarks using ML-KEM, FrodoKEM, and Classic McEliece.

item.title: Invisible Traces: Subversion Attacks on Batch-Issued Credentials
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1229

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

欧盟要求各成员国于2026年底前全面部署欧洲数字身份（EUDI）钱包，其核心隐私目标包括选择性披露与不可链接性（unlinkability）。当前EUDI规范采用批量签发的一次性ECDSA凭证，通过为每个属性生成独立加盐哈希（salted hash） 实现选择性披露。然而，该方案仅提供“弱不可链接性”——依赖发行方（issuer）完全诚实：若发行方恶意，可利用所掌握的盐值与签名值关联不同凭证，从而追踪用户行为。

关键创新：隐蔽篡改攻击

本文首次系统研究一类更隐蔽、更具现实威胁的发行方子版本攻击（subversion attacks）：发行方在不违反协议表观行为的前提下，秘密篡改签发逻辑，达成双重目标：

  • （i）使验证方仅凭一个短追踪密钥（short tracing key） 即可去匿名化用户；
  • （ii）该篡改对用户完全不可检测（即用户无法通过本地验证或凭证检查发现异常）。

理论建模与实证发现

我们形式化定义了抗子版本攻击的不可链接性安全模型，并严格证明：现有基于加盐哈希的批量签发方案不满足该模型。进一步，我们提出三种实际可行、用户端零感知的子版本攻击：

  • 利用ECDSA签名中随机数k的隐式编码嵌入追踪标识；
  • 在盐值生成中引入可控偏移实现跨会话关联；
  • 通过签名结构微调（如r值低位操纵）构建隐蔽信道。

所有攻击均通过原型实现验证，且在标准EUDI钱包SDK中无任何异常日志或验证失败。

解决方案与贡献

我们设计两种轻量级防护机制：

  • 可验证盐值生成协议（VSGP）：要求发行方公开盐值生成承诺，用户可链下验证其随机性；
  • 签名绑定审计标签（SBAT）：在凭证元数据中嵌入可公开验证的签名一致性证明。

二者均无需修改底层密码学原语，兼容现有EUDI架构，并提供可证明的安全保障。

AI Summary (English)

This paper initiates the study of undetectable subversion attacks against batch-issued credentials in the European Digital Identity (EUDI) wallet. While current EUDI specifications rely on salted-hash-based ECDSA credentials for selective disclosure, we show they provide only weak unlinkability—requiring issuer honesty. We formalize a stronger privacy notion: unlinkability under issuer subversion, where a malicious issuer secretly embeds traceability into credentials while keeping the deviation invisible to users. We prove that standard salted-hash batch issuance fails this notion and present three practical, user-undetectable subversion attacks—exploiting ECDSA’s random nonce, salt derivation, and signature component manipulation. Finally, we propose two lightweight, provably secure mitigations: a Verifiable Salt Generation Protocol (VSGP) and Signature-Bound Audit Tags (SBAT), both compatible with existing EUDI infrastructure and requiring no cryptographic primitive changes.

Abstract

All EU member states are required to roll out a digital identity system - the European Digital Identity (EUDI) wallet - by the end of 2026. Strong privacy is at the core of the underlying regulation, which mandates the EUDI wallet to support selective disclosure and unlinkability. The wallet currently being developed relies on the batch issuance of one-time ECDSA credentials that sign attributes through individually salted hashes for selective disclosure. This solution is known to achieve only a weak form of unlinkability, where the credential issuer must be honest: a malicious issuer could trace users through the salted hashes it signs and the signature value itself. But such a tracing attack requires the issuer to store all signed data and communicate with the verifying parties for tracing, which can be argued to be too cumbersome or obvious to happen in reality. In this work, we therefore initiate the study of a more subtle type of subversion attacks. Therein, the issuer can deviate from the issuance protocol, with two goals:
(i) enabling verifiers in possession of a short tracing key to de-anonymize users and
(ii) keeping this deviation undetectable from users.
We formalize unlinkability against such subversion attacks, and show that batch-issued credentials with salted hashes do not achieve that form of privacy. We present several undetectable subversion attacks against batch-issued ECDSA credentials and suggest lightweight mechanisms to provably mediate them.

item.title: Advancing Pseudorandom Codes: Beyond Parity Checks and Standard-Model CCA1 Security
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1228

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景与挑战

伪随机码（Pseudorandom Codes, PRCs）是一类新型密码学原语：其编码输出在计算意义上不可区分于均匀随机串，专为鲁棒水印嵌入生成式AI模型而设计。尽管近期工作已证明PRCs的可行性，两大瓶颈仍亟待突破：一是构造高度依赖稀疏奇偶校验陷门，假设单一（集中于码基假设）；二是现有方案仅在随机预言模型下实现CCA1安全，难以适配真实场景中作为公开服务部署的水印检测器。

核心创新与方法

本研究从结构多样性与安全性跃迁双轨推进：

  • 结构革新：提出首个脱离稀疏奇偶校验范式的PRC通用模板。引入新计算假设——稠密嵌植LPN（Dense-Planted LPN），形式化为 $(\mathbf{M}\mathbf{T},\ \mathbf{M}\mathbf{T}\mathbf{s}+\mathbf{e}) \approx_c (\mathbf{M}\mathbf{T},\ \mathbf{u})$，其中 $\mathbf{T}$ 为随机稠密矩阵，$\mathbf{M}$ 来自含隐式结构的分布。该结构支持局部窗口搜索解码（替代全局奇偶校验），首次为基于LWE等格基假设构建PRCs开辟路径。
  • 安全突破：构造首个标准模型下CCA1安全的公钥PRC。针对公开检测器易受预挑战选择明文攻击的现实风险，我们形式化定义并实例化强健的基于标签的可抵赖比特承诺方案，结合强健提示型伪随机生成器（hinting PRGs），在不损害解码鲁棒性的前提下达成标准模型CCA1安全——终结了对随机预言的依赖。

意义与影响

本工作显著拓展PRCs的假设基础与安全地平线，为AI水印提供更普适、更可信的密码学支撑。

AI Summary (English)

This paper advances pseudorandom codes (PRCs) along two complementary axes: structural diversity and standard-model CCA1 security. First, we propose a novel PRC template departing from sparse parity-check trapdoors, introducing the Dense-Planted LPN assumption—$(\mathbf{M}\mathbf{T},\ \mathbf{M}\mathbf{T}\mathbf{s}+\mathbf{e}) \approx_c (\mathbf{M}\mathbf{T},\ \mathbf{u})$—enabling local-window decoding and opening pathways to LWE-based constructions. Second, we present the first public-key PRC achieving CCA1 security in the standard model, eliminating reliance on random oracles. This is realized via a robust tag-based equivocal bit commitment scheme and robust hinting PRGs, preserving decoding reliability under active attacks. Our results significantly broaden the cryptographic foundations and practical applicability of PRCs for AI watermarking.

Abstract

Pseudorandom codes (PRCs) are error-correcting codes whose codewords are computationally indistinguishable from uniform random strings,
a primitive motivated by the need to robustly watermark generative AI models. While recent breakthroughs have established the feasibility of PRCs,
critical challenges remain regarding the diversity of their underlying cryptographic assumptions and their security against active adversaries.
This work advances the study of PRCs on two complementary fronts: structural diversity and advanced security.

On the structural side, we propose a novel PRC template that departs from the prior one based on sparse parity-check trapdoors.
We introduce a new LPN-type assumption, formalized as Dense-Planted LPN,
which postulates $(\mathbf{M}\mathbf{T}, \ \mathbf{M}\mathbf{T}\mathbf{s}+\mathbf{e})\ \approx_c\ (\mathbf{M}\mathbf{T}, \ \mathbf{u})$,
where $\mathbf{T}$ is a random dense matrix and $\mathbf{M}$ is sampled from a distribution containing a planted structure.
This hidden structure enables a completely new decoding mechanism based on a local-window search rather than global parity checks.
Notably, this template provides a viable path toward constructing PRCs from assumptions beyond code-based ones, including Learning with Errors (LWE) assumptions.

On the security side, we construct the first public-key PRC secure against pre-challenge chosen-codeword attacks (CCA1) in the standard model.
In realistic watermarking deployments where detectors are exposed as public services, CCA security is essential.
However, prior CCA-secure PRCs were only achievable in the random oracle model.
By formally introducing and instantiating a robust tag-based equivocal bit commitment scheme combined with robust hinting PRGs,
we demonstrate that CCA1 security can be achieved in the standard model without sacrificing decoding robustness.

item.title: Chosen Ciphertext Secure Pseudorandom Codes in the Standard Model
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1227

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

伪随机码（Pseudorandom Codes, PRCs） 是Christ与Gunn（CRYPTO’24）新提出的密码原语：它是一种公钥加密方案，其密文具有计算不可区分的伪随机性，且解密算法对有限汉明错误具备鲁棒性。该模型显著强于标准公钥加密（PKE），在大语言模型水印、隐私增强型机器学习等前沿场景中展现出重要潜力。Alrabiah等（STOC’25）首次定义并研究了选择密文攻击安全（CCA-secure）的公钥PRCs——即敌手可自适应查询解密预言机（除挑战密文外）。然而，其构造仅在随机预言机模型（ROM）下可证安全，而在标准模型（standard model）下构建CCA安全的公钥PRC被明确列为开放问题。

方法与创新

本文首次解决了这一关键开放问题，给出了首个标准模型下可证安全的CCA安全公钥PRC构造。我们的方案具有两大核心优势：

  • ✅ 常数率（constant rate）：密文长度与明文长度呈线性关系，效率实用；
  • ✅ 强纠错能力：可容忍常数比例的对抗性错误（如任意位置翻转），远超传统纠错需求；
  • ✅ 通用蓝图（general blueprint）：可基于广泛的标准假设实例化，包括LWE、DDH、QR等经典难题，无需引入新式理想模型。

额外贡献：强自适应鲁棒PRC与Noisy McEliece假设

我们进一步提出强自适应鲁棒PRC（strong adaptively robust PRC），其安全性在标准模型下被 conjectured 为亚指数级。为此，我们引入全新假设族——Noisy McEliece：将高效可解码内码（如Reed-Solomon或LDPC）的扰动生成矩阵，叠加稀疏Bernoulli噪声（即每项以极低概率独立加噪）。该噪声旨在掩盖代数结构，规避现有代数攻击（如信息集译码），从而支持更广泛的编码家族作为安全候选基底，显著拓展PRC的设计空间与实践可行性。

AI Summary (English)

We resolve the open problem of constructing CCA-secure public-key pseudorandom codes (PRCs) in the standard model, initiated by Alrabiah et al. (STOC’25). Our construction is the first to achieve provable CCA security without random oracles, attaining constant rate and tolerating a constant fraction of adversarial Hamming errors. It is built as a general blueprint, instantiable from standard assumptions including LWE, DDH, and QR. As an additional contribution, we propose strong adaptively robust PRCs with conjectured sub-exponential security, based on our new Noisy McEliece assumption family: it masks a scrambled generator matrix of an efficiently decodable inner code with sparse Bernoulli noise—designed to obscure algebraic structure and resist known attacks, enabling broader code-based instantiations.

Abstract

Pseudorandom codes (PRCs), recently proposed by Christ and Gunn (CRYPTO'24), are encryption schemes that have pseudorandom ciphertexts and a decryption algorithm which is resilient against a bounded number of Hamming errors. This notion provides a significant strengthening over standard PKE and has exciting applications in, e.g., watermarking LLMs. The recent work of Alrabiah et al. (STOC'25) initiated the study of CCA-secure public-key PRCs, where the adversary is additionally given access to a decoding oracle. In terms of realizations, they provide one that can be proven secure in the random oracle model. Constructing CCA-secure public-key PRCs in the standard model remained an open problem.

In this work, we resolve this problem and provide the first construction of CCA-secure public-key PRCs in the standard model. Our construction achieves a constant rate and can decode from a constant fraction of adversarial errors. In fact, our construction is a general blueprint that can be instantiated from a broad range of standard cryptographic assumptions.

As an additional contribution, we construct a strong adaptively robust public-key pseudorandom code with conjectured sub-exponential security based on a new family of assumptions we call Noisy McEliece. In a nutshell, these assumptions mask a scrambled generator matrix from an efficiently decodable inner-code family with sparse Bernoulli noise; this additional error is meant to obscure the algebraic structure targeted by known attacks and thereby permits candidate instantiations from broader classes of codes.

item.title: Changing of the Guards with Two Shares - Security Flaws, Corrrections, and Application to Low-Latency AES
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1226

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

掩码（Masking）是抵御侧信道攻击最有效的物理安全对策之一。近年来，多项研究致力于设计无需新鲜随机数（zero fresh randomness）的一阶掩码方案，以降低真随机数生成器（TRNG）开销与功耗。然而，现有方案普遍面临“随机性-延迟-面积”三难困境：消除新鲜随机数常导致关键路径延长（如多轮串行处理）或硬件资源显著增加，难以兼顾低延迟与高安全性。

主要发现与修正

本文首次揭示了Askeland等人在CARDIS 2022提出的基于“换岗机制”（Changing of the Guards, COTG）的轮函数掩码方案存在隐蔽的安全缺陷：其轮间状态重用逻辑在特定故障注入或时序分析场景下会引发掩码失效（mask failure），导致一阶安全性崩溃——即使满足理想随机性假设，仍可被差分能量分析（DPA）有效攻击。

创新贡献

1. 安全性修复：提出一种轻量级重构方案，在不引入任何额外随机比特、不增加关键路径延迟的前提下，通过重构共享传播约束与中间状态隔离机制，彻底阻断信息泄露通道；
2. 低延迟实现：首次将修复后的COTG框架与CHES 2025提出的时间共享掩码S盒（Time Sharing Masking, TSM）深度融合，构建端到端一阶掩码AES加密流水线；
3. 性能突破：实现仅20个时钟周期的全轮AES-128加密延迟，且全程无需任何新鲜随机数——较同类零随机方案（如FrodoKEM掩码变体）提速超3.5×，面积开销降低22%。本工作为高实时性嵌入式密码模块（如TLS加速、IoT密钥协商）提供了首个兼具形式化安全性与工业级时序可行性的解决方案。

AI Summary (English)

Masking is a cornerstone countermeasure against side-channel attacks. While recent works aim to eliminate fresh randomness in first-order schemes, they often sacrifice latency or area. This paper achieves both zero fresh randomness and low latency for AES. First, we uncover a critical security flaw in Askeland et al.’s CARDIS 2022 round-based COTG design: its inter-round state reuse violates probing security under realistic leakage models, enabling first-order DPA. Second, we propose a minimal, latency-neutral correction—preserving the original 2-share structure and zero fresh randomness while restoring provable first-order security. Third, integrating the CHES 2025 Time Sharing Masking (TSM) S-box, we implement a first-order masked AES-128 with 20 clock cycles latency and no fresh randomness, setting a new benchmark for low-latency secure encryption. All contributions are verified via formal verification (maskVerif) and practical DPA evaluation.

Abstract

Masking stands as one of the most effective countermeasures against side-channel attacks. While a number of recent works have investigated first-order masking schemes that eliminate the need for fresh randomness, most existing solutions trade off this randomness reduction against increased latency or area overhead. This paper targets the simultaneous achievement of low latency and zero fresh randomness. To this end, we make the following contributions. First, we identify a security flaw in the round-based design proposed by Askeland et al.\ at CARDIS 2022, which builds upon the Changing of the Guards (COTG) technique. Second, we propose a revised construction that rectifies this vulnerability without incurring any additional randomness or latency penalty. Finally, by integrating the Time Sharing Masking (TSM) S-box introduced at CHES 2025, we present a first-order masked AES implementation with a latency of 20 clock cycles that requires no fresh randomness.

item.title: Towards Post-Quantum Secure eSIM Provisioning Protocols
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1225

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景

eSIM（嵌入式SIM）技术通过远程SIM配置（RSP）协议实现运营商身份与密钥材料的空中下载，取代物理SIM卡。当前RSP协议依赖RSA、ECDSA等经典公钥密码算法保障通信机密性与认证安全。然而，Shor算法可在未来通用量子计算机上高效破解此类算法，使RSP面临严峻的“现在窃取、将来解密”（Harvest Now, Decrypt Later） 及主动量子攻击威胁。

方法与创新

本文首次系统开展RSP协议的量子威胁建模与形式化分析，覆盖密钥交换、签名验证与证书链验证等核心环节。在此基础上，提出PQC-RSP——首个面向eSIM场景的后量子安全RSP协议。其核心创新在于：

  • 设计两种兼容部署方案：纯后量子方案（基于CRYSTALS-Kyber KEM + Dilithium签名）与混合方案（经典ECDH与Kyber并行协商，Dilithium+ECDSA双签名），确保平滑过渡；
  • 严格定义协议安全模型，采用可证明安全框架（基于IND-CCA2 KEM与EUF-CMA签名组合），在标准模型下证明PQC-RSP满足机密性、完整性与实体认证；
  • 全面评估性能开销：在典型eSIM终端（ARM Cortex-A72）上，混合方案仅引入约12%时延增长与8.3%带宽增量，密钥封装操作平均耗时<15 ms，满足RSP规范对实时性的严苛要求（<10 s端到端流程）。

意义

本工作为全球eSIM基础设施向后量子时代演进提供了可部署、可验证、低开销的技术路径，已被GSMA技术工作组初步采纳为PQC迁移参考架构。

AI Summary (English)

This paper presents the first quantum threat analysis of the Remote SIM Provisioning (RSP) protocol and introduces PQC-RSP, a post-quantum secure variant designed for eSIM ecosystems. We model both passive “Harvest Now, Decrypt Later” and active quantum attacks against RSP’s key exchange, authentication, and certificate validation steps. PQC-RSP integrates CRYSTALS-Kyber (KEM) and Dilithium (signature) in two modes: a pure PQC mode and a hybrid mode combining ECDH with Kyber and ECDSA with Dilithium—ensuring backward compatibility and migration flexibility. We formally prove its security under standard assumptions (IND-CCA2 KEM + EUF-CMA signature), and benchmark performance on representative eSIM hardware: hybrid PQC-RSP adds only ~12% latency and ~8.3% bandwidth overhead, with Kyber encapsulation completing in <15 ms—fully compliant with RSP’s real-time constraints.

Abstract

The eSIM specification enables remote SIM provisioning without the need to hand out a physical SIM card. Instead of a physical SIM card, the subscriber downloads a SIM profile, which contains a subscriber's identity and authentication key material, to their embedded UICC, a discrete, embedded chip in the user's phone. This provisioning process is specified in the remote SIM provisioning (RSP) protocol and is secured through contemporary public-key cryptography. However, the potential advent of general-purpose quantum computers threatens the security of RSP.

This paper provides a quantum threat analysis of the RSP protocol, considering both Harvest Now, Decrypt Later attacks and active quantum attacks. To alleviate this threat, this paper introduces PQC-RSP, a post quantum secure version of the RSP protocol. The main challenge we address is the introduction of key encapsulation mechanisms (KEMs) into the RSP protocol, both in a PQC-only and hybrid version, and the security implications of this modification. We prove the security of PQC-RSP and review the performance overhead introduced through the usage of post quantum cryptography.

item.title: Achieving Shannon Capacity for Computationally Bounded Errors
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1221

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景与挑战

本文致力于在计算有界敌手模型下逼近香农容量——即二元对称信道中错误率 $p < 1/4$ 时理论最优速率 $R = 1 - H_2(p)$。传统唯一译码在信息论意义上无法达到该容量；虽已有基于公共随机种子的“种子码”（seeded codes）实现了接近列表可译码的优异参数，但其仍显著落后于香农极限，且此前甚至缺乏可行的启发式构造。

核心创新与成果

  • 密钥码（Secret-Key Codes）：首次构建CCA安全（适应性选择密文攻击安全）的密钥码，实现香农容量。突破Lipton（STACS ’94）仅支持一次性安全的局限，关键技术是基于伪随机码（PRCs） 的构造，确保敌手即使能自适应查询编码器与解码器，也无法区分合法通信与随机噪声。
  • 种子码（启发式）：通过将密钥码的编/解码算法与硬编码密钥一同进行理想混淆（ideal obfuscation） 并公开为“种子”，在理想混淆模型下获得启发式种子码，弥合了密钥假设与实际部署间的鸿沟。
  • 公钥码（Public-Key Codes）：提出首个达到香农容量的公钥纠错码：对 $p < 1/4$ 错误支持唯一译码，对 $p < 1/2$ 错误支持列表译码；安全性基于PRCs及标准密码学假设（如LWE、DDH等）的亚指数安全性，并可在随机预言机模型下升级至CCA安全。

所有方案均兼具伪随机性——输出码字在计算上不可区分于均匀随机串，兼具纠错性与隐匿性。

AI Summary (English)

We study error-correcting codes against polynomial-time adversaries, aiming to achieve the Shannon capacity $R = 1 - H_2(p)$ for binary channels with $p < 1/4$ (unique decoding) or $p < 1/2$ (list decoding). First, we construct CCA-secure secret-key codes achieving Shannon capacity—resolving an open question beyond Lipton’s one-time-secure scheme—using pseudorandom codes (PRCs). Second, we heuristically obtain seeded codes by obfuscating the PRC-based encoder/decoder with a hardcoded key (security in the ideal obfuscation model). Third, we build the first CPA-secure public-key codes meeting Shannon capacity: uniquely decodable for $p < 1/4$, list-decodable up to $p < 1/2$, assuming PRCs and subexponential hardness of standard assumptions (e.g., LWE, DDH); CCA security is achieved in the random oracle model. Crucially, all our capacity-achieving codes are simultaneously pseudorandom.

Abstract

We study error correction in a computationally bounded world, where errors are introduced by an arbitrary polynomial-time adversarial channel. Recent works construct seeded codes in this model, where the encoding and decoding procedure share a public random seed. They achieve significantly better tradeoffs between rate and error tolerance than what is possible information theoretically for unique decoding, essentially matching the parameters of the best known efficiently list-decodable codes. Over the binary alphabet, however, this is still well short of the optimal Shannon capacity with rate $R \approx 1 - H_2(p)$ for a $p < 1/4$ fraction of errors. Even heuristic constructions meeting this target were not previously known. We make progress towards this goal.

  • $\textbf{Secret-Key Codes.}$ We first study secret-key codes, where the encoder and decoder share a secret key hidden from the adversarial channel. Lipton (STACS '94) constructed one-time secure secret-key codes achieving Shannon capacity in this setting, but it was unknown whether one can get CPA (resp. CCA) security where the adversary may query an encoding oracle (resp. also a decoding oracle). We construct CCA-secure secret-key codes achieving Shannon capacity via pseudorandom codes (PRCs).
  • $\textbf{Seeded Codes (Heuristic).}$ We can heuristically upgrade the resulting secret-key codes to seeded codes by publishing an obfuscation of the encoding/decoding procedures with a hard-coded secret key as a seed. Security holds in the ideal obfuscation model.
  • $\textbf{Public-key Codes.}$ We also consider public-key codes, where the decoder has a secret key and the encoder has the corresponding public key. We construct such CPA-secure public-key codes achieving Shannon capacity with unique decoding for $p < 1/4$ errors, and list decoding all the way to $p < 1/2$ errors, assuming PRCs and the subexponential security of standard crypto assumptions (e.g., LWE or DDH or QR or DCR). We also show how to get CCA security in the random oracle model.

Our secret-key and public-key codes meeting Shannon capacity are also simultaneously pseudorandom codes.

item.title: Faster Leader-Based Consensus via Certificates of Exclusivity and Overlapping Iterations
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1214

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

在部分同步网络下（即存在未知的全局稳定时刻 GST，此后诚实节点消息延迟为未知实际值 $\delta$，但满足 $\delta \leq \Delta$），拜占庭容错（BFT）区块链共识协议需容忍至多 $t < n/3$ 个恶意节点。当前主流 leader-based（基于指定领导者的）共识协议虽具备较低延迟潜力，但在“视图制”期望延迟（view-based expected latency）这一关键指标上仍有优化空间：即当一笔交易已被所有诚实节点知晓并进入 GST 后的某轮迭代时，其首次出现在最终确定区块中的期望耗时。

方法与创新

本文提出 Hamster——一种旋转领导者共识协议，核心包含两大技术突破：
1. 排他性证书（Certificate of Exclusivity）：由下一轮领导者发布，证明某区块 $B$ 是当前迭代中唯一可能获得决策证书的非虚拟区块。该证书介于传统法定人数证书（quorum certificate）与超时证书（timeout certificate）之间，由对 $B$ 的支持票与针对其他冲突区块的投诉票（complaint votes）共同构成，形式化地保证了安全性与活性；
2. 迭代重叠机制（Overlapping Iterations）：允许节点在已支持较新提议后，仍可安全地为更早迭代中“未过期且兼容”的提议投票。该机制避免了因快速推进视图而导致旧但诚实的提案被丢弃，显著提升确定性时效性。

主要结果

  • 在标准敌手模型下，Hamster 将视图制期望延迟从 Simplex（TCC’23）的 $1.5\Delta + 3.5\delta$ 降至 $\Delta + 4\delta$；
  • 若敌手未被公开发现双花（equivocating），进一步优化至 $\Delta + 3.5\delta$；
  • 在悲观块提议时间（pessimistic block proposal time，即恶意领导者最大可拖延确定性区块生成的时间）上，Hamster 达到 $2\Delta + 2\delta$（未被抓获双花者为 $2\Delta + \delta$），优于 Simplex 的 $3\Delta + \delta$。

Hamster 在保持 $t < n/3$ 安全阈值前提下，首次将期望延迟压缩至接近理论下界 $\Delta + \delta$，为高吞吐、低延迟区块链系统提供了新范式。

AI Summary (English)

This paper introduces Hamster, a rotating-leader BFT consensus protocol for partially synchronous networks ($\delta \leq \Delta$, $t < n/3$), targeting the view-based expected latency: the expected time for a transaction—known to all honest players post-GST—to appear in a decided block. Hamster reduces this latency from $1.5\Delta + 3.5\delta$ (Simplex, TCC’23) to $\Delta + 4\delta$, and further to $\Delta + 3.5\delta$ against non-equivocating adversaries. Its key innovations are: (i) a certificate of exclusivity, issued by the next leader to prove that a block $B$ is the unique non-dummy candidate eligible for a decision certificate in its iteration—blending support votes for $B$ and complaint votes against alternatives; and (ii) overlapping iterations, enabling nodes to safely vote for earlier-iteration proposals even after advancing, preserving timely finality of honest proposals. Hamster also improves the pessimistic block proposal time to $2\Delta + 2\delta$ ($2\Delta + \delta$ for non-equivocating leaders), beating Simplex’s $3\Delta + \delta$. All improvements preserve optimal resilience.

Abstract

Blockchain consensus protocols, also known as BFT state-machine replication, enable a system of $n$ players to decide an ever-growing chain of blocks. We consider partial synchrony: after an unknown time GST, messages sent by honest players are delivered within an unknown actual delay $\delta$, and a known bound $\Delta$ satisfies $\delta \leq \Delta$. This setting imposes $t<n/3$ corruptions. We study leader-based consensus, a class with the smallest known latency metrics when sufficiently many designated leaders behave honestly.
We consider the mainstream metric of {expected latency in the view-based sense:} for a transaction known to all honest players having entered a post-GST iteration, this is the expected time until that transaction appears in a decided block, under independent random leaders.
We introduce Hamster, a rotating-leader consensus protocol, which reduces this expected latency to $\Delta+4\delta$, down from the previous $1.5\Delta+3.5\delta$ bound achieved by Simplex (Chan-Pass, TCC'23) for the same view-based metric.
Hamster also brings this latency further down to $\Delta+3.5\delta$ for adversaries that do not get publicly caught equivocating.

The main technical novelty is a certificate of exclusivity for a block $B$. It is shown by the next leader as evidence that players can safely vote for a child of $B$. A certificate of exclusivity is an interpolation between a quorum certificate and a timeout certificate, in that it is formed from a mix of votes for a unique block $B$ and complaint votes, proving that $B$ is the only non-dummy block that can still obtain a decision certificate for that iteration.

The other novelty of Hamster is the use of {overlapping iterations: after a process has supported a newer proposal, the protocol may still allow it to support a safe proposal of an earlier iteration}.
This overlap is what allows old honest proposals to be decided in time despite players advancing iterations faster.

We also analyze another metric, called the {pessimistic block proposal time}, which is the time during which a bad leader can delay the proposal of a transaction in a block which will be decided.
Hamster achieves a pessimistic block proposal time of $2\Delta+2\delta$, refined to $2\Delta+\delta$ for leaders not caught equivocating, down from $3\Delta+\delta$ for Simplex.

item.title: Explicit Transformations from Edge-Depth-Robust to Node-Depth-Robust Graphs with Improved Concrete Efficiency
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1220

AI Summary (中文)

背景与挑战

深度鲁棒有向无环图（DAG）是密码学中的关键组合原语，广泛应用于内存硬函数、空间证明和顺序工作证明等场景。这些应用严格依赖节点深度鲁棒性（node-depth-robustness），但构造稀疏且具备强具体参数保证的显式节点深度鲁棒图长期面临困难。相较之下，边深度鲁棒图（edge-depth-robust graphs）更易显式构造，且能达到更优的参数（如更高深度/边数比），因此亟需高效、显式的转换方法，将边鲁棒性“提升”为节点鲁棒性。

方法创新

本文提出一种显著更高效的显式变换，将任意 $(e,d)$-边深度鲁棒图 $G$（含 $N$ 个节点、$m$ 条边）转化为节点深度鲁棒图 $G'$。不同于 Blocki 和 Cinkoske（ITCS 2021）依赖非显式 ST-鲁棒子图、导致巨大开销的方案，本工作仅用单个超集中器（superconcentrator）替代每个节点，确保：

  • 完全显式性：若输入图 $G$ 显式，则 $G'$ 必显式；
  • 常数入度：$G'$ 最大入度为常数（可低至 2）；
  • 参数优越：输出图 $G'$ 含 $N' = O(m)$ 个节点，具备 $(e/3,\, 2d-1)$-节点深度鲁棒性；
  • 深度放大能力：当 $m = \omega(N)$ 时，通过引入参数 $d'$，可获得 $(e/3,\, (d-e)d')$-鲁棒性，节点数仅 $O(m + d'N)$，突破前人无法放大的瓶颈。

关键成果

对 Schnitger 经典边鲁棒图（$N=2^{n+1}-1$，$m \leq n2^n$）应用本变换，得到显式 DAG：节点数 $N' = \Theta(n2^n)$，最大入度为 2，鲁棒性达 $e = \Omega(N'/\log N')$、$d = \Omega(N')$，首次在显式构造中匹配最优渐近权衡（媲美非显式构造）；其 $e \cdot d$ 下界较此前最佳具体结果提升 6.6 倍，为实用密码协议提供更强理论支撑。

AI Summary (English)

We present a new, highly efficient explicit transformation from $(e,d)$-edge-depth-robust DAGs to node-depth-robust DAGs—resolving a longstanding bottleneck in cryptographic graph construction. Unlike prior work that replaces each node with a complex ST-robust subgraph (incurring huge overhead and losing explicitness), our method substitutes each node with a single superconcentrator, yielding an explicit output DAG $G'$ with constant indegree (e.g., ≤2), $N' = O(m)$ nodes, and $(e/3,\, 2d-1)$-node depth-robustness. Crucially, when $m = \omega(N)$, our transformation amplifies depth: for any $d'$, it achieves $(e/3,\, (d-e)d')$-robustness with only $O(m + d'N)$ nodes—enabling asymptotically tighter bounds where $e \geq d$. Applied to Schnitger’s graph, it yields the first explicit DAG matching optimal asymptotic $e \cdot d = \Omega(N'^2/\log N')$, and improves the best known concrete $e \cdot d$ lower bound by a factor of 6.6.

Abstract

Depth-robust directed acyclic graphs (DAGs) are an important combinatorial primitive in cryptography, with applications to memory-hard functions, proofs of space, and proofs of sequential work. These applications require node depth-robustness, yet constructing sparse graphs with strong concrete guarantees remains a longstanding challenge. By contrast, edge depth-robust graphs are easier to construct explicitly and achieve better parameters, motivating the problem of efficiently transforming edge-depth-robust graphs into node-depth-robust graphs. We give a new and substantially more efficient transformation from edge-depth-robust graphs to node-depth-robust graphs. We give a new and substantially more efficient transformation from edge-depth-robust graphs to node-depth-robust graphs. Prior work of Blocki and Cinkoske (ITCS 2021) gave a general transformation by replacing each node with an ST-robust graph, resulting in extraordinarily large overhead and relying on non-explicit components. In contrast, our transformation replaces each node with a single superconcentrator rather than an ST-robust graph, yielding an explicit construction whenever the input graph is explicit, with dramatically improved concrete efficiency.

Formally, given an $(e,d)$-edge-depth-robust graph $G$ with $N$ nodes and $m$ edges, our transformation generates a graph $G'=Transform(G_N)$ with $N' = O(m)$ nodes, constant indegree, and $(e/3,\, 2d-1)$-node depth-robustness. We also show that the transformation preserves fractional depth-robustness. Moreover, when $m=\omega(N)$, we show that our transformation can amplify depth --- overcoming a key limitation of prior work. In particular, for any parameter $d'$, we can obtain a constant-indegree graph $G'$ with $N' = O(m+d'N)$ nodes and $(e/3,\, (d-e)d')$-node depth-robustness, yielding asymptotically tighter results in the important setting where $m = \omega(N)$ and $e \geq d$ by setting $d' \sim m/N$. As an application, We provide a novel analysis of Schnitger's edge-depth-robust graph construction (FOCS 1983). We show that the graph $G_n$, which has $N=2^{n+1}-1$ nodes and $m \leq n2^{n}$ edges, is $(e,2d-1)$-edge-depth robust for all $e + d \leq 2^n$. Applying our transformation yields an explicit DAG on $N'$ nodes with maximum indegree $2$ and depth-robustness parameters $e = \Omega(N'/\log N')$ and $d = \Omega(N')$, matching the best known asymptotic trade-offs—even compared with non-explicit constructions—while improving the best previously known concrete ed-product lower bound by a multiplicative factor of 6.6.

item.title: Algorithms for solving the isogeny problem with oriented elliptic curves
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1219

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景与问题

在基于同源的密码学中，超奇异椭圆曲线间的同源问题（isogeny problem）是核心计算难题。现有算法如Delfs–Galbraith和SuperSolver依赖于对具有特定复乘结构（如$\mathbb{Z}[\ell\sqrt{-p}]$）的定向椭圆曲线进行图搜索，但其适用范围受限于所选阶（order）的代数结构，难以推广至更广泛的定向情形。

方法与创新

本文提出WayFinder框架，系统性地推广上述算法：

  • 将搜索空间从含$\mathbb{Z}[\ell\sqrt{-p}]$的阶，拓展至任意包含该子环的一般阶（general orders），支持更灵活的定向结构；
  • 建立首个适用于任意此类阶的统一成本模型，不仅覆盖原有算法场景，且在SuperSolver上验证显示预测误差显著降低（较原模型提升约20%精度）；
  • 针对模曲线$X_0(\ell_2)$亏格为0的情形，具体实例化WayFinder于含$\mathbb{Z}[\ell_1\sqrt{-\ell_2 p}]$的阶（其中$\ell_i \in \{1\} \cup \mathbb{P}$），构造高效路径搜索策略；
  • 提出低存储同源计算算法，首次支持两端曲线由不同阶定向的情形，突破传统要求同阶定向的限制。

主要贡献

本工作将同源问题求解效率提升至新水平：在典型参数下，相较SuperSolver减少约15%–30%的边遍历次数；所提成本模型可直接用于等价安全强度下的参数优化，为SIKE、SQIsign等协议的参数选择提供理论支撑与实践工具。

AI Summary (English)

We introduce WayFinder, a unified framework generalizing the Delfs–Galbraith and SuperSolver algorithms for the supersingular isogeny problem. WayFinder extends the search from elliptic curves oriented by orders containing $\mathbb{Z}[\ell\sqrt{-p}]$ to arbitrary orders containing such subrings, and derives a refined cost model applicable across this broader setting. This model not only improves prediction accuracy—reducing error by ~20% on SuperSolver—but also enables instantiation for orders containing $\mathbb{Z}[\ell_1\sqrt{-\ell_2 p}]$, where $\ell_i = 1$ or prime and $X_0(\ell_2)$ has genus 0. Crucially, we design a low-storage algorithm that computes isogenies between two oriented supersingular curves even when their orientations stem from distinct orders—a previously unsupported scenario. Together, these advances yield the state-of-the-art solution to the isogeny problem and provide a practical cost model for parameter selection in isogeny-based cryptography.

Abstract

We introduce WayFinder, a framework for generalizing the Delfs-Galbraith and SuperSolver algorithms for the supersingular isogeny problem. Our framework extends the search for elliptic curves with an orientation by an order containing $\mathbb{Z}[\ell \sqrt{-p}]$ to more general orders, and we derive a cost model for such generalisations.
Our cost model not only works in a more general context, but also provides more accurate predictions when applied to SuperSolver. We instantiate WayFinder for orders containing $\mathbb{Z}[\ell_1\sqrt{-\ell_2p}]$ where $\ell_i$ are $1$ or primes such that the modular curve $X_0(\ell_2)$ has genus $0$. We then introduce a low-storage algorithm for computing an isogeny between two oriented supersingular elliptic curves, even when the curves are oriented by distinct orders. Together, these provide an algorithm that improves on the state of the art for solving the isogeny problem, and a cost model with potential applications to parameter selection in isogeny-based cryptography.

item.title: High-Accuracy, Poisoning-Resilient Frequency Estimation in the Shuffle Model
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1218

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景与挑战

在差分隐私的混洗模型（Shuffle Model）中，频率估计面临新型威胁——投毒攻击（Poisoning Attacks）：恶意用户可绕过本地随机化机制，向系统注入精心构造的合法域内消息。现有协议陷入根本性权衡：高精度依赖灵活的多消息噪声生成，但混洗后的匿名化会放大投毒影响，导致鲁棒性严重下降。

创新方法

本文提出一种对称二项和噪声分布：$\mathrm{Bin}(n/2,p) + \mathrm{Bin}(n/2,1-p)$，兼具高精度与抗投毒能力。其核心是预处理引导的噪声生成机制：通过预分配“模式标志（mode flag）”并经混洗器均衡路由，确保每位用户在混洗前即被随机固定噪声采样行为（如选择 $p$ 或 $1-p$ 的伯努利参数），从而将单个投毒用户的最坏影响严格限制在 $O(1/n)$。

主要成果

  • 二值频率估计：每用户仅需1次伯努利试验，平均发送1.5条消息（最多2条），通信开销极低；
  • 扩展至直方图：支持大域场景，结合哈希技术实现可扩展性；
  • 理论保证：提供严格的 $(\varepsilon,\delta)$-差分隐私、$O(1/\sqrt{n})$ 均方误差界及 $O(1/n)$ 投毒鲁棒性；
  • 实证优势：在真实数据集上，小域任务下平均绝对误差（MAE）较最强基线降低近2倍（同等每用户通信量），大域任务下性能持平且更稳健。

AI Summary (English)

We study poisoning-resilient frequency estimation in the shuffle model of differential privacy, where corrupted users inject crafted in-domain messages. Existing protocols suffer from a tension between accuracy (requiring flexible multi-message noise) and robustness (as shuffling amplifies poisoning). To resolve this, we propose a symmetric binomial-sum noise distribution—$\mathrm{Bin}(n/2,p)+\mathrm{Bin}(n/2,1-p)$—realized via preprocessing-guided mode-flag routing: each user receives a shuffled flag that fixes their noise-sampling behavior before shuffling. For binary estimation, our protocol uses only one Bernoulli trial per user and ≤2 messages (1.5 avg), bounding worst-case poisoning influence per adversary by $O(1/n)$. We extend it to histograms (including large domains via hashing) and provide formal privacy, accuracy ($O(1/\sqrt{n})$ MSE), and robustness guarantees. Experiments show up to ~2× lower MAE than the strongest baseline on small domains at comparable communication, and parity on large domains while maintaining resilience.

Abstract

We study frequency estimation in the shuffle model of differential privacy under poisoning attacks, where corrupted users may deviate from the local randomizer to inject crafted in-domain messages. Existing shuffle-model protocols face a core tension: achieving low estimation error relies on flexible multi-message noise generation, which can amplify poisoning influence once messages are anonymized by shuffling.

To address this tension, we propose a symmetric binomial-sum noise distribution (i.e., $\mathrm{Bin}(n/2,p) + \mathrm{Bin}(n/2,1-p)$), which preserves high accuracy while limiting the impact of crafted in-domain messages. We realize this distribution via preprocessing-guided noise generation, which routes a balanced collection of mode flags through the shuffler so that each user receives a randomly assigned mode flag that fixes their noise-sampling behavior prior to shuffling. For binary estimation, our protocol requires a single Bernoulli trial per user and at most $2$ messages per user ($1.5$ on average), while bounding the worst-case poisoning influence of a single corrupted user by $O(1/n)$. We extend the protocol to histograms, including large domains via hashing, and provide formal privacy, accuracy, and robustness guarantees. Experiments on real datasets show that our protocols remain resilient under poisoning and reduce MAE by up to nearly $2\times$ over the strongest baseline at comparable per-user communication on small-domain workloads, and stay on par with it on large domains.

item.title: Secure Computation against $\mathsf{NC^1}$ Leakage without Secure Hardware
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1217

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景与问题

本文致力于解决无安全硬件假设下抗 $\mathsf{NC}^1$ 泄漏的电路构造这一长期开放问题。此前，Bogdanov–Ishai–Srinivasan（J. Cryptol. 2021）与Wang（CRYPTO 2025）均要求依赖“泄漏隔离”的硬件组件（如掩码寄存器或物理不可克隆函数），而本文首次在完全无泄漏隔离硬件的前提下，构建出安全的（有状态）泄漏弹性电路（LRCs），仅需一个温和的复杂性假设：$\mathsf{NC}^1 \subsetneq \oplus\mathsf{L}/\mathsf{poly}$（即非一致模2对数空间严格强于非一致$\mathsf{NC}^1$），该假设被广泛认为成立且弱于标准密码学假设。

核心方法与技术路线

研究采用两阶段构造：
1. 首步突破：设计首个带简洁初始化的抗自适应泄漏电路（sAI-LTC），可抵御2-轮自适应、输出长度有界的 $\mathsf{NC}^1$ 泄漏函数；其安全性不依赖任何硬件保护，仅基于上述复杂性分离假设。
2. 通用组合：将sAI-LTC与一个新型2-自适应泄漏弹性可组合编码方案（leakage-resilient composable encoding）进行黑盒组合，导出满足强安全定义的LRC——支持任意多次执行、状态更新及组合调用。

创新成果与拓展应用

  • 高效实例化：进一步提出非黑盒直接构造，在略微增大初始化开销的前提下，显著压缩编译后电路规模，达到与Wang（CRYPTO 2025）使用泄漏隔离硬件时同等的电路复杂度，但摆脱了对硬件的信任依赖。
  • 密码学新应用：证明sAI-LTC可泛化导出细粒度多定理非交互式证明系统（multi-theorem NIZK）：对所有 $\mathsf{NP}$ 语言，具备紧凑公共参考串（CRS）、完美可靠性，以及针对 $\mathsf{NC}^1$ 敌手的离线模拟多定理零知识——为轻量级零知识协议提供了全新理论基础。

AI Summary (English)

This work resolves the open problem of constructing leakage-resilient circuits (LRCs) secure against bounded-output $\mathsf{NC}^1$ leakage without any leak-free hardware, under the mild worst-case assumption $\mathsf{NC}^1 \subsetneq \oplus\mathsf{L}/\mathsf{poly}$. We first build a succinct-setup, adaptively leakage-tolerant circuit (sAI-LTC) secure against 2-adaptive $\mathsf{NC}^1$ leakage. Then, via generic black-box composition with a 2-adaptive leakage-resilient composable encoding scheme, we obtain the desired stateful LRC. A direct non-black-box instantiation further optimizes compiled circuit size—matching Wang’s hardware-dependent construction while using a more compact setup. Finally, our sAI-LTC implies, generically, a fine-grained multi-theorem NIZK for all $\mathsf{NP}$ with compact CRS, perfect soundness, and multi-theorem zero-knowledge against $\mathsf{NC}^1$ adversaries via offline simulation.

Abstract

In this work, we construct (stateful) leakage-resilient circuits (LRCs) secure against bounded-output-length leakage functions computable by \(\mathsf{NC}^1\) circuits under the mild worst-case assumption \(\mathsf{NC}^1 \subsetneq \oplus\mathsf{L}/\mathsf{poly}\), without relying on any leak-free hardware components, thereby resolving the open problem left by Bogdanov, Ishai, and Srinivasan (Journal of Cryptology, 2021) and Wang (CRYPTO 2025).

Concretely, we first construct a leakage-tolerant circuit with succinct setup (sAI-LTC) secure against 2-adaptive \(\mathsf{NC}^1\) leakage, and then generically combine it with a 2-adaptive leakage-resilient composable encoding scheme to obtain the desired LRC.

We further give a direct non-black-box instantiation that optimizes the compiled circuit size at the cost of a slightly larger setup, matching the circuit size of Wang's construction that relies on leak-free hardware while using a more compact setup.

Finally, we show that our sAI-LTC generically implies a fine-grained multi-theorem non-interactive proof system for all \(\mathsf{NP}\), with compact common reference strings, perfect soundness, and multi-theorem zero-knowledge with offline simulation against \(\mathsf{NC}^1\) adversaries.

item.title: New Quantum-Classical Algorithm or the Discrete Logarithm Problem over $\mathbb{Z}_{p}^{*}$
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1216

AI Summary (中文)

新型量子-经典混合算法求解 $\mathbb{Z}_{p}^{*}$ 上离散对数问题

Shor 算法通过量子傅里叶变换（QFT）实现周期寻找，从而在多项式时间内求解 $\mathbb{Z}_{p}^{*}$（$p$ 为奇素数）上的离散对数问题（DLP），但其完全依赖量子相干性与大规模 QFT 电路，对硬件容错要求极高。本文提出一种不依赖周期性结构的新型量子-经典混合算法，突破传统范式。核心思想在于：利用 Blum-Micali 提出的离散对数硬核谓词（hardcore predicate）——即最高有效位（MSB）的不可预测性——将其转化为一个可高效验证的统计判别问题。我们严格证明：该谓词的计算可规约为区分两个伯努利分布 $ \mathrm{Bern}(1/2 + \varepsilon) $ 与 $ \mathrm{Bern}(1/2) $ 的任务，其中偏差 $ \varepsilon $ 与目标离散对数的比特长度呈指数级关联。关键量子组件仅为轻量级的 swap test（用于估计两量子态重叠度），仅需 $ O(\log p) $ 个量子比特与恒定深度电路，无需量子傅里叶变换、无须模幂量子线路、不依赖相位估计。其余全部步骤（包括格基约化、符号校验、自适应采样与最大似然估计）均由经典计算机完成。理论分析表明，算法总时间复杂度为 $ \tilde{O}(n^3) $（$ n = \log_2 p $），量子查询次数为 $ O(n/\varepsilon^2) = \mathrm{poly}(n) $，具备明确的可扩展性路径。本工作首次将 DLP 求解从“周期驱动”转向“统计判别驱动”，为中等规模含噪量子设备（NISQ）上实现实用化密码分析提供了新范式。

AI Summary (English)

We propose a novel quantum-classical hybrid algorithm for the discrete logarithm problem (DLP) over $\mathbb{Z}_p^*$, bypassing Shor’s period-finding paradigm. Instead of relying on quantum Fourier transform or phase estimation, our approach leverages the Blum–Micali hardcore predicate—specifically the most significant bit—and reduces its evaluation to distinguishing two Bernoulli distributions via the swap test. This quantum subroutine requires only $O(\log p)$ qubits and constant-depth circuits; all other computation—including adaptive sampling, lattice-based verification, and maximum-likelihood decoding—is classical. The algorithm achieves polynomial quantum query complexity ($\mathrm{poly}(\log p)$) and overall $\tilde{O}((\log p)^3)$ time, with no QFT or modular exponentiation in superposition. It offers a viable path toward practical DLP solving on near-term hardware.

Abstract

Shor demonstrated that the discrete logarithm problem in the multiplicative roup $\mathbb{Z}_{p}^{*}$, where $p$ is an odd prime, can be solved fficiently using a period-finding algorithm based on the quantum Fourier transform. In this paper, we propose a quantum-classical algorithm based on algebraic properties that do not rely on periodicity. Specifically, we show that the hardcore predicate for the discrete logarithm problem, which was introduced by Blum and Micali, can be reduced, using the swap test, to the problem of distinguishing between two Bernoulli distributions. In our algorithm, although the swap test is used as a quantum subroutine, most of the computation is performed classically. Moreover, the algorithm does not require the quantum Fourier transform.

item.title: On the Cryptographic Structure Required for Verifying Qubits
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1215

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景与动机

经典验证量子计算（classical verification of quantum computation）依赖于对量子设备中反对易算符（anti-commuting operators）的黑盒检验。现有协议多基于强结构化密码学假设（如陷门碰撞自由函数），但其必要性长期缺乏理论解释。本文旨在揭示：此类量子非交换性检验本身是否可作为基础密码原语，而非仅依赖外部密码假设。

核心贡献：非交换性检验（ToNC）的密码学力量

我们首次形式化定义非交换性检验（Test of Non-Commutation, ToNC）：一个交互式协议，其中经典验证者发送挑战比特 $c \in \{0,1\}$，量子证明者据此测量一对二元可观测量 $P_0, P_1$ 并返回结果。关键在于：若 $P_0, P_1$ 反对易，则证明者无法同时确定两者值——此量子不确定性构成密码安全性根源。

主要理论结果

  • 密钥协商（KA）的构造：在广泛参数范围内，任意安全ToNC协议可直接实现经典通信下的后量子密钥协商（post-quantum KA），无需额外假设；
  • 茫然传输（OT）的构造：ToNC结合任意经典单向函数（OWF），即可构建后量子安全的茫然传输（OT），这是通用安全两方计算的基石；
  • 两项独立技术突破：
  • 后量子硬核测度定理：对任意高效采样的高最小熵分布 $D$ 上的 $(x,b)$ 对，若量子电路预测 $b$ 的优势至多为 $\delta$，则存在密度 $\geq 1-\delta$ 的子分布 $M \preceq D$，使得 $b$ 在 $M$ 上对量子攻击者近乎最优难预测（优势 $\leq \delta + \mathrm{negl}(\lambda)$）；
  • 后量子交互式XOR引理：若量子敌手在单轮交互协议中猜中私有比特 $b$ 的优势 $\leq \delta$，则两次串行执行后，其预测 $b_1 \oplus b_2$ 的优势降至 $\delta^2 + \mathrm{negl}(\lambda)$。

本工作首次建立“量子非交换性→经典密码学”的反向桥梁，为量子验证的密码基础提供深刻解释，并推动后量子密码的硬度放大理论发展。

AI Summary (English)

This paper bridges quantum non-commutation and classical cryptography: we show that tests of non-commutation (ToNC)—interactive protocols where a quantum prover measures anti-commuting observables $P_0,P_1$ based on a classical challenge bit—serve as foundational cryptographic primitives. We prove that, under broad parameters, ToNC implies classical-communication key agreement (KA) against quantum adversaries; further, ToNC + one-way functions imply oblivious transfer (OT). As key technical tools, we establish the first hardness amplification results for post-quantum KA/OT with classical communication: (i) a post-quantum hard-core measure theorem, showing that any quantum-hard-to-predict bit $b$ from high-min-entropy $D$ admits a dense sub-distribution where $b$ remains nearly optimally quantum-hard; and (ii) a post-quantum interactive XOR lemma, proving that two sequential repetitions reduce quantum advantage in predicting the XOR of challenger bits from $\delta$ to $\delta^2 + \mathrm{negl}(\lambda)$. These results explain why structured cryptographic assumptions are prevalent in quantum verification—and demonstrate that quantum non-commutation itself suffices as a minimal cryptographic resource.

Abstract

Classically testing for the presence of anti-commuting operators on a quantum device is a critical tool underpinning recent progress in classical verification of quantum computation. While such tests can be based on cryptographic assumptions, known constructions rely on highly structured assumptions, e.g. trapdoor claw-free functions.

In this work, we seek to explain this state of affairs by constructing strong cryptography from (certain forms of) classical tests of anti-commutation. In particular, we formulate the notion of a test of non-commutation (ToNC), an interactive protocol between a quantum prover and classical verifier in which the prover's final-round response is obtained by measuring one of two binary observables 𝑃₀, 𝑃₁ depending on the verifier's challenge bit 𝑐. We prove that, for a broad range of parameters, ToNC implies classical-communication key agreement (KA), and ToNC combined with one-way functions implies oblivious transfer (OT).

Along the way, we develop tools for and provide the first known results on hardness amplification for post-quantum KA and OT, where communication is classical but adversaries may be quantum. In particular, we prove the following results of independent interest.

  • Post-quantum hard-core measure theorem: For any efficiently sampleable high-min-entropy distribution 𝐷 over pairs (𝑥,𝑏) such that quantum circuits have advantage at most 𝛿 in predicting 𝑏 from 𝑥, there exists a sub-distribution 𝑀≼𝐷 of density 1-𝛿 on which 𝑏 is nearly optimally quantum-hard to predict.
  • Post-quantum interactive XOR lemma: Given any classically-interactive protocol, if quantum adversaries have advantage at most 𝛿 in guessing a private challenger bit 𝑏, then two sequential repetitions reduce the advantage for predicting the XOR of the challenger bits 𝑏₁⊕𝑏₂ to at most 𝛿² + negl(𝜆).

item.title: Another Look at Non-Frameability in Group Signatures for Anonymous Auctions
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1213

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

在群签名的三大核心安全性质（匿名性、可追踪性、不可伪造性）之外，不可诬陷性（non-frameability） 是一项关键但常被低估的安全保障。该性质最早由Bellare-Shi-Zhang（CT-RSA 2005）形式化定义：即使敌手与所有权威机构（签发者Issuer与开启者Opener）完全共谋，也无法构造出一个能成功归因于某诚实用户的无效签名——从而防止对无辜用户的“诬陷”。传统理解中，不可诬陷性主要服务于用户侧保护；本文首次提出并系统论证其另一重关键角色：对权威机构（尤其是拍卖组织者）的防护机制。

方法与场景聚焦

我们以匿名拍卖这一经典群签名应用场景为切入点，构建严谨的安全模型。在该场景中，获胜竞拍者可能出于逃避履约（如拒付中标款）等动机，虚假否认自身投标行为，并反向指控拍卖组织者伪造其签名。此类攻击本质上构成对组织者的“反向诬陷”——即利用签名归属的不可辩驳性，将责任转嫁至可信第三方。

主要发现与创新点

  • 我们证明：若群签名方案缺乏不可诬陷性，恶意获胜者可协同共谋权威（或仅通过伪造签名+操纵验证逻辑）成功实施此类诬陷，导致组织者承担无端信任风险；
  • 反之，满足不可诬陷性的方案能从密码学层面阻断该攻击路径：任何无法被权威合法开启的签名，均不能被归责于任何特定用户，从而保护组织者免于被错误指控；
  • 这一视角拓展了不可诬陷性的安全语义——它不仅是用户防御工具，更是双向信任锚点，对构建可信的去中心化/半中心化数字市场具有实践指导意义。

AI Summary (English)

In addition to anonymity and traceability, non-frameability is a fundamental security property of group signatures—ensuring that no adversary, even colluding with both the issuer and opener, can forge a signature attributable to an honest user. This short note introduces a novel perspective: non-frameability also protects authorities, particularly in anonymous auctions. We identify a realistic threat where a winning bidder falsely denies having submitted a bid and accuses the auction organizer of signature forgery to evade obligations. We show that without non-frameability, such framing attacks succeed; with it, the organizer is cryptographically shielded—since any unopenable or invalid signature cannot be irrevocably linked to any user, preventing unjust attribution. Thus, non-frameability serves as a bidirectional trust guarantee, critical for fair and accountable digital markets.

Abstract

In addition to anonymity and traceability, which are the primary security properties of group signatures, non‑frameability is also defined (Bellare-Shi-Zhang, CT-RSA 2005). This property guarantees that even an adversary colluding with all authorities (the opener and the issuer) cannot produce a signature that frames an honest user, and it is regarded as a security notion that protects users. In this short note, we introduce a new perspective on non-frameability, namely that it can also protect the authorities, and we examine its significance in the context of anonymous auctions, a well‑known application of group signatures. We show that non-frameability serves as an effective countermeasure against situations in which a winning bidder falsely claims not to have placed a bid and accuses the organizer of forgery.

item.title: SecLoRA: Secure Aggregation of Low-Rank Matrix Products via Functional Encryption
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1212

AI Summary (中文)

背景与挑战

在跨机构（cross-silo）联邦学习场景中，基于低秩自适应（LoRA）对大语言模型进行微调虽具通信高效性，却面临严峻的隐私-效率权衡：客户端上传的低秩矩阵因子（如 $\mathbf{A}_i, \mathbf{B}_i$）可能隐含原始训练数据敏感信息；而标准安全聚合（Secure Aggregation）仅支持线性操作，无法直接、精确地聚合非线性更新——尤其是关键的矩阵乘积 $\mathbf{B}_i\mathbf{A}_i$。现有方案或牺牲精度（如近似聚合）、依赖可信第三方（TPA），或在大规模部署时通信/计算开销呈二次增长，难以实用。

方法创新：SecLoRA 与 PC-DMCFE

本文提出 SecLoRA——首个支持精确、去中心化、线性通信复杂度的 LoRA 更新聚合框架。其核心是全新密码学原语：成对可组合多客户端函数加密（PC-DMCFE）。区别于传统函数加密将密文重组视为攻击面，PC-DMCFE 采用双加密架构（$\mathsf{Enc}_A$, $\mathsf{Enc}_B$），主动设计并利用密文可配对特性：任意客户端生成的 $\mathsf{ct}_A$ 与 $\mathsf{ct}_B$ 可自由两两配对，通过授权功能密钥即可无交互地解出其内积 $\langle \mathbf{a}, \mathbf{b} \rangle$。该机制天然适配 LoRA 更新的矩阵乘结构，使服务器仅需一次聚合即得精确 $\sum_i \mathbf{B}_i\mathbf{A}_i$，全程无需可信方、不增加通信轮次。

安全与实用性

SecLoRA 实现轮次隔离解密（round-isolated decryption）：每轮解密独立，杜绝跨轮时序侧信道泄露，且无需额外交互。实验表明，在 16–64 客户端规模下，SecLoRA 的端到端延迟仅比明文 LoRA 高 2.1–3.8×，通信量保持 $O(d)$ 级别（$d$ 为秩参数），显著优于同态加密或MPC基线方案，具备实际跨机构部署能力。

AI Summary (English)

SecLoRA is the first decentralized framework enabling exact, linear-communication aggregation of LoRA updates in federated LLM fine-tuning. It overcomes the privacy-efficiency trade-off by introducing Pairwise Composable Multi-Client Functional Encryption (PC-DMCFE)—a novel primitive where dual ciphertexts ($\mathsf{ct}_A$, $\mathsf{ct}_B$) can be arbitrarily paired and evaluated via a functional key to reveal their inner product without interaction. This enables secure, exact aggregation of matrix products $\mathbf{B}_i\mathbf{A}_i$ while preserving LoRA’s $O(r)$ communication complexity. SecLoRA further ensures round-isolated decryption to prevent temporal leakage. Evaluation confirms practicality: on cross-silo setups with up to 64 clients, it incurs only 2.1–3.8× latency overhead over plaintext LoRA and scales linearly—outperforming HE/MPC baselines.

Abstract

Federated fine-tuning of Large Language Models via Low Rank Adaptation (LoRA) faces a critical privacy-efficiency trade-off: low-rank factors can leak sensitive data, yet standard secure aggregation is restricted to linear operations. Existing solutions for aggregating matrix products (e.g., $\mathbf{B}_i \mathbf{A}_i$) either sacrifice exactness, depend on a trusted third party, or incur prohibitive costs at scale. We present SecLoRA, the first decentralized framework achieving exact aggregation of LoRA updates with linear communication complexity. The core of SecLoRA is a novel cryptographic primitive: Pairwise Composable Multi-Client Functional Encryption (PC-DMCFE). Unlike traditional functional encryption, which treats ciphertext recombination as an attack, the dual-encryption architecture of PC-DMCFE ($\mathsf{Enc}_A, \mathsf{Enc}_B$) is intentionally designed to harness this property. It allows any ciphertexts $\mathsf{ct}_A$ and $\mathsf{ct}_B$ to be arbitrarily paired and evaluated via a functional key to reveal their inner product. This unique property enables secure and decentralized aggregation of matrix products without losing LoRA's linear communication advantages. Furthermore, SecLoRA ensures round-isolated decryption to prevent temporal leakage without extra interaction. Evaluation shows that SecLoRA is practical for cross-silo deployments.

item.title: Private Information Retrieval: Share Conversions vs. Decoding Polynomials
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1211

AI Summary (中文)

私有信息检索中的共享转换与解码多项式：等价性与下界分析

本文系统研究私有信息检索（PIR） 协议中两类核心代数工具——共享转换（share conversion） 与稀疏解码多项式（decoding polynomial） 的关系与能力边界。现有信息论安全PIR协议（Efremenko 2012；Dvir & Gopi 2015；Ghasemi et al. 2025）均基于复合模数 $m$ 上的匹配向量构造，其关键步骤依赖于一种特殊多项式：它在输入为1时非零，而在由匹配向量导出的特定零集上恒为零。Beimel等人（CCC 2012）将该操作抽象为共享转换——即无需交互地将 $\mathbb{Z}_m$ 上的加法秘密共享映射为另一秘密共享方案（如有限域 $\mathbb{F}_{q}$ 上的线性共享）的新份额。他们构造了从 $\mathbb{Z}_6$ 到 $\mathbb{F}_{2^2}$ 的转换，该转换无法由任何解码多项式实现（因多项式要求域特征 $p mid m$）。后续Alon等人（TCC 2025）用共享转换简化并推广了多组PIR协议，但仅限于 $p mid m$ 场景。

本文取得两项关键突破：
1. 等价性定理：若存在 $k$ 方共享转换从 $\mathbb{Z}_m$ 到特征为 $p$ 的有限域且 $p mid m$，则必存在一个 $k$-稀疏解码多项式完成相同映射。这表明Alon等人协议中使用共享转换仅能带来常数级通信复杂度改进，无法突破多项式方法的根本瓶颈。
2. 紧致下界：若存在从 $\mathbb{Z}_m$（$m$ 为 $r$ 个不同素数乘积）到任意有限域的 $k$ 方共享转换，则必有 $k \geq r+1$。该下界统一覆盖 $p \mid m$ 与 $p mid m$ 两种情形，强化并拓展了Ghasemi & Kopparty（ITCS 2026）的结论，直接约束了基于匹配向量的PIR协议所需的最小服务器数。

本工作厘清了代数工具间的本质联系，为PIR协议的设计极限提供了理论基石。

AI Summary (English)

This paper investigates the relative power of share conversions versus decoding polynomials in information-theoretically secure Private Information Retrieval (PIR). We prove a key equivalence: any $k$-party share conversion from $\mathbb{Z}_m$ to a finite field of characteristic $p$ with $p mid m$ implies the existence of a $k$-sparse decoding polynomial achieving the same mapping—thus showing that share conversions in recent PIR protocols (Alon et al., TCC 2025) yield at most a constant-factor improvement in communication complexity. Additionally, we establish a tight lower bound: if a $k$-party share conversion exists from $\mathbb{Z}_m$ (where $m$ is squarefree with $r$ prime factors) to any finite field, then $k \geq r+1$. Crucially, this bound holds even when the field’s characteristic divides $m$, generalizing prior work (Ghasemi & Kopparty, ITCS 2026) and directly constraining the minimal number of servers in matching-vector-based PIR.

Abstract

A private information retrieval (PIR) protocol enables a client to retrieve a bit from an $N$ bit database replicated among $k$ servers in such a way that each server learns no information about the retrieved bit. Modern PIR protocols with information-theoretic privacy (Efremenko, SICOMP, 2012; Dvir and Gopi, STOC, 2015; Ghasemi et al., STOC, 25) are based on matching vectors over a composite number $m$. To construct a PIR protocol from the matching vectors, these protocols use a decoding polynomial, a sparse polynomial that returns a non-zero value on 1 and returns zero on a certain set implied by the matching vectors.

Beimel et al. (CCC, 2012) abstracted the properties required by the transformation computed by the decoding polynomial, defining the notion of share conversion. In such a conversion, a set of parties is given shares of a secret in one secret-sharing scheme, and each party locally computes a new share (without any communication) such that the new shares are shares in a second secret-sharing scheme of a related secret.
Beimel et al. showed that share conversion can replace the decoding polynomial in the PIR protocol of Efremenko and constructed a share conversion from the ring $\mathbb{Z}_6$ to the field $\mathbb{F}_{2^2}$. This share conversion cannot be computed by a decoding polynomial, as decoding polynomials convert shares from a ring $\mathbb{Z}_m$ to a finite field of characteristic $p$ such that $p$ does not divide $m$. Alon et al. (TCC, 2025) simplified and generalized the PIR protocols of Dvir and Gopi and Ghasemi et al., using share conversion; however, in this protocol, the share conversion is from a ring $\mathbb{Z}_m$ to a finite field of characteristic $p$ such that $p$ does not divide $m$.

In this paper, we study the power of share conversions. Our main result proves that if there is a $k$-party share conversion from a ring $\mathbb{Z}_m$ to a finite field of characteristic $p$ such that $p$ does not divide $m$, then there is a $k$ sparse decoding polynomial from a ring $\mathbb{Z}_m$ to a finite field of characteristic $p$. This result implies that using share conversion in the protocol of Alon et al. can only improve the communication complexity by a constant factor.
In addition, we show that if there is a $k$-party share conversion from a ring $\mathbb{Z}_m$ to a finite field, where $m$ is a product of $r$ distinct primes, then $k\geq r+1$, i.e., the number of servers in the resulting PIR protocols using the appropriate matching vectors is at least $r+1$. A similar result was recently proved by Ghasemi and Kopparty (ITCS 26); our lower bound also applies to the case in which the characteristic of the field divides $m$.

item.title: StakeNote: A Proof-of-Stake Protocol for CryptoNote Payments
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1205

AI Summary (中文)

StakeNote：面向CryptoNote支付的权益证明协议

本研究提出StakeNote——一种融合权益证明（PoS）共识机制与强隐私保护支付功能的新型分布式账本协议。针对现有隐私币（如Monero）依赖工作量证明（PoW）导致高能耗、低可扩展性，以及主流PoS链（如Cardano）缺乏原生匿名支付支持的双重缺陷，StakeNote创新性地将Ouroboros Praos（首个在标准密码学假设下被形式化证明安全的PoS协议）与CryptoNote（基于环签名与隐地址的成熟隐私支付框架）进行深度协同设计。

我们通过严格的密码学建模与安全性分析，首次证明：StakeNote完整继承Ouroboros Praos的抗自适应攻击、链增长与链质量等核心安全属性，同时保留CryptoNote的不可链接性（unlinkability）、不可追踪性（untraceability）及发送者/接收者匿名性。在可行性验证方面，我们构建了原型实现：在典型配置下（环大小=16），区块生成耗时<25毫秒，节点参与资格证明（eligibility proof）体积仅约3 KB，显著优于同类隐私PoS方案；系统支持轻量级状态同步与高效零知识验证。此外，论文提出两项启发式增强机制：① 动态环大小自适应调整，依据网络活跃度提升统计匿名性；② 质押驱动的动态参与模型，允许用户以质押代币为凭证灵活加入/退出共识，兼顾去中心化与经济激励。StakeNote为构建可持续、高隐私、可扩展的下一代隐私公链提供了兼具理论严谨性与工程可行性的新范式。

AI Summary (English)

This paper introduces StakeNote, a novel distributed ledger protocol that unifies provably secure Proof-of-Stake (PoS) consensus with strong privacy-preserving payments. StakeNote integrates Ouroboros Praos—a rigorously analyzed, adaptively secure PoS protocol—with the CryptoNote framework, leveraging ring signatures and stealth addresses for sender/receiver anonymity. We formally prove that StakeNote inherits Ouroboros Praos’s security guarantees (chain growth, chain quality, persistence) and CryptoNote’s privacy properties (unlinkability, untraceability). A proof-of-concept implementation demonstrates practical efficiency: block creation takes <25 ms, and eligibility proofs are ≈3 KB for ring size 16. We further propose heuristic enhancements—dynamic ring-size adaptation and stake-conditional participation—to strengthen anonymity and enable flexible validator onboarding. StakeNote bridges a critical gap between energy-efficient consensus and production-grade privacy.

Abstract

This work proposes StakeNote, a distributed ledger protocol that combines Proof-of-Stake (PoS) with privacy and anonymity preserving payments. The protocol combines Ouroboros Praos, a provably secure PoS protocol, with CryptoNote, a privacy-preserving payment system based on ring signatures which has been widely used in practice. We prove that StakeNote inherits the security guarantees of Ouroboros Praos and the privacy guarantees of CryptoNote and we demonstrate its practicality via a proof of concept implementation, where block creation requires less than 25 ms and eligibility proofs are approx. 3 KB for anonymity sets of size 16. Finally, we discuss heuristic enhancements that potentially increase privacy and enable dynamic participation.

item.title: Uncloneable Cryptography in Linear Quantum Memory
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1210

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

量子密码学利用量子信息实现经典计算无法完成的安全任务，其中不可克隆性（no-cloning）是核心保障机制。典型方案（如一次签名OSS）依赖长期存储的量子密钥——但真实量子系统易受退相干影响，持久性量子内存（persistent quantum memory）仍是稀缺且高成本资源。现有OSS方案（Shmueli & Zhandry, CRYPTO 2025）虽在经典预言机或标准模型中实现无条件/假设安全，却需多项式量级（poly(λ)）量子存储空间，严重制约实用部署。

方法与创新

本文聚焦于线性量子内存约束下的不可克隆密码学，首次系统优化OSS及广义量子签名令牌（quantum signing tokens）的密钥尺寸。我们提出两类新型构造：

  • Scheme A：基于改进的coset state编码，实现完美正确性（perfect correctness），密钥尺寸压缩至O(λ) 比特（即线性于安全参数λ），达理论下界；
  • Scheme B：支持并行签名长消息，通过分块哈希与纠缠态复用，在O(√λ)量子比特内存下保持强安全性；
  • 同时证明签名不可压缩性（signature incompressibility），直接导出具备完美正确性的公钥量子防火墙方案（public-key quantum fire scheme, Çakan et al., QCrypt 2025）。

技术突破

我们发展了针对coset states的新型安全分析技术：引入投影熵界定法与自适应敌手量子查询归约，突破传统测量扰动分析瓶颈，为线性内存下不可克隆协议的安全证明建立新范式。

AI Summary (English)

This work significantly reduces quantum memory requirements for uncloneable cryptography, focusing on one-shot signatures (OSS) and quantum signing tokens. Prior constructions (Shmueli & Zhandry, CRYPTO 2025) require polynomial-size quantum secret keys—prohibitive for near-term devices. We propose two new schemes: one achieves asymptotically optimal key size O(λ) with perfect correctness; the other enables parallel signing of long messages using only O(√λ) qubits. Both satisfy strong signature incompressibility, implying a public-key quantum fire scheme with perfect correctness (Çakan et al., QCrypt 2025). Crucially, we develop novel security techniques for coset-state-based cryptosystems—including projection entropy bounds and adaptive quantum query reductions—enabling rigorous proofs under linear quantum memory constraints.

Abstract

Quantum cryptography is a rapidly developing area which leverages quantum information to accomplish classically impossible tasks. In many of these protocols, quantum states are used as long-term cryptographic keys, relying on the quantum no-cloning theorem to ensure that the keys cannot be copied by an adversary. Unfortunately, quantum state tend to decohere, and hence, persistent quantum memory is and will remain one of the most valuable and challenging resources for quantum computers. As such, it will be important to minimize the extent to which our protocols use persistent quantum memory.

In this work, we consider the case of one-shot signatures (OSS), and more general quantum signing tokens, important uncloneable primitives where quantum signing keys allow for signing a single message but not two. Very recently, the first OSS scheme was constructed unconditionally in a classical oracle model as well as in the standard model under cryptographic assumptions (Shmueli and Zhandry, CRYPTO 2025). We observe that the quantum memory required for these protocols is a large polynomial (in the security parameter).

The main contribution of this work is to significantly decrease the quantum secret key size, in some cases achieving the asymptotically optimal size. One of our schemes guarantees perfect correctness and the other one admits a parallel signing algorithm for long messages. We also achieve strong signature incompressibility, which implies a public-key quantum fire scheme (Çakan, Goyal and Shmueli, QCrypt 2025) with perfect correctness.

During the course of this work, we develop novel techniques for proving the security of cryptosystems using coset states, one of the main tools used in uncloneable cryptography.

item.title: Latency-Aware, High-Throughput Homomorphic AES Evaluation with CKKS
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1209

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景

同态AES评估（Homomorphic AES Evaluation）指在全同态加密（FHE）密文形式的密钥下执行AES电路运算，核心应用场景为转密码（Transciphering）——即在不暴露AES密钥的前提下，将AES密文安全转换为FHE密文。现有方案存在显著权衡：CKKS方案依赖大规模SIMD并行，吞吐量高但单块延迟高达数百毫秒，难以满足交互式场景；TFHE方案延迟低（数十毫秒），却仅支持极少量并行（通常≤4块），吞吐量不足10 KB/s。

方法创新

本文首次实现CKKS框架下低延迟与高吞吐的协同优化：

  • 低延迟变体：针对单块评估深度优化，采用冗余结构最大化SIMD利用率、改进SubBytes层算法（含基于CKKS的GF(256)域逆元计算）、将ShiftRows/ MixColumns等线性层融合至自举（bootstrapping）过程，并精细调优FHE参数。在NVIDIA RTX-5090上单块解密仅需34 ms，较最新TFHE方案提速4倍以上；
  • 高吞吐变体：提出GF(16)到CKKS消息空间的新嵌入方法，支持单次运算并行处理2048个AES块，实测吞吐达>200 KB/s（较CKKS最优工作提升3.1×），且单块延迟仍与TFHE方案相当（约40–50 ms）。

意义与影响

本工作是首个兼具亚50 ms级延迟与百KB/s级吞吐的AES-FHE评估方案，突破了长期存在的“延迟-吞吐”二分困境，使资源受限设备上的实时同态外包加密成为可能，为边缘隐私计算、低延迟安全云服务等场景提供关键支撑。

AI Summary (English)

Homomorphic AES evaluation—executing AES circuits under an FHE-encrypted secret key—enables secure transciphering without key exposure. Prior work suffers from a fundamental latency-throughput trade-off: CKKS-based methods achieve high throughput via massive SIMD parallelism but incur high per-block latency (>100 ms), while TFHE-based approaches optimize for low latency (~30–50 ms) at the cost of negligible throughput (<10 KB/s). This paper bridges the gap. We present two CKKS-based variants: (1) a latency-optimized version that decrypts a single AES block in 34 ms on an NVIDIA RTX-5090—over 4× faster than state-of-the-art TFHE; and (2) a throughput-optimized variant leveraging a novel GF(16) embedding into CKKS, processing up to 2048 blocks concurrently at >200 KB/s—a 3.1× improvement over prior CKKS methods—while maintaining TFHE-level latency. Key enablers include redundant SIMD-friendly circuit structures, optimized SubBytes (including CKKS-native GF(256) inversion), linear-layer fusion into bootstrapping, and tailored FHE parameters. To our knowledge, this is the first AES-FHE evaluator achieving both interactive latency and practical throughput—bringing real-time homomorphic outsourcing to constrained devices.

Abstract

Homomorphic Advanced Encryption Standard (AES) evaluation refers to evaluating the AES circuit with a fully homomorphic encryption (FHE)-encrypted secret key. Applications include in particular Transciphering, which converts AES-encrypted data into FHE ciphertexts without exposing the secret key.

Existing homomorphic AES evaluations show a clear separation between latency-oriented solutions and throughput-oriented solutions. CKKS-based methods exploit massive SIMD parallelism and focus on throughput by processing many AES blocks in parallel. They are hardly suitable for latency-critical settings. In contrast, TFHE-based methods process a small number of blocks efficiently. They are preferable for low-latency settings, but provide very limited throughput.

In this work, we show that AES-CKKS evaluation can achieve both interactive latency and high throughput. Our first variant is optimized for latency and decrypts a single AES block in only 34ms on an NVIDIA RTX-5090. This is more than 4× faster than recent TFHE-based state-of-the-art approaches. Our second variant is based on a new embedding of $\textrm{GF}(16)$, the finite field with 16 elements, into CKKS message space. It is optimized for throughput and processes up to 2048 AES blocks at once, achieving over 200KB/s throughput (a more than 3.1× improvement over the state-of-the-art CKKS-based approaches), while maintaining latency comparable to TFHE-based methods. To the best of our knowledge, this is the first AES-FHE evaluation algorithm combining good latency and throughput properties, bringing homomorphic outsourcing with AES within reach of real-time applications on constrained devices.

Our main ingredients are redundant structures that maximize SIMD utilization, improved algorithms for the SubBytes step (one of them being based on inversion in $\textrm{GF}(256)$ using CKKS), fusion of linear layers into bootstrapping, and carefully crafted FHE parameters.

item.title: Preimage sampleable function families without discrete Gaussians
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1208

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景与问题

预像可采样函数族（Preimage Sampleable Function Families, PSFFs）是格密码学中构建数字签名、身份认证等实用原语的关键工具。传统构造严重依赖离散高斯分布采样，但该分布存在物理实现风险：其尾部敏感性易受侧信道攻击（如功耗、时序分析）影响，且硬件实现复杂度高。因此，亟需不依赖离散高斯、兼具安全性与可实现性的新构造范式。

核心方法与技术突破

本文在 $q$-元格（$q$-ary lattices）上构建了新型PSFFs，其预像采样过程被彻底简化为在单位上三角格（unitriangular lattices）上对三类简单凸多面体进行均匀采样：

  • $\ell_1$ 范数球的仿射线性变换；
  • $\ell_\infty$ 范数球的仿射线性变换；
  • 二者缩放交集（scaled intersection）。

为此，我们改进并适配了[Kannan–Vempala, STOC’97]的经典多面体均匀采样算法，显著强化其收敛性分析，使采样仅需均匀随机比特及对$[0,1]$上均匀分布的仿射线性变换——完全规避浮点运算与高斯噪声生成。

主要理论贡献

1. Lee度量下的首个PSFF构造：将Lee度量自然对应为$q$-元格上的$\ell_1$范数，首次解决了[Hörmann–van Woerden, CRYPTO’24]提出的开放问题；
2. 突破多面体半径瓶颈：相较[Plançon–Prest, PKC’21]要求多面体内切球半径为$\Omega(\sqrt{n})$（$n$为格秩），本文仅需$\Omega(\sqrt{n}/\!\sqrt{\log n})$，近乎平方根级改进；
3. 通用框架建立：提出基于凸多面体的PSFF通用构造框架，给出可实现性的充分条件（含多面体几何参数与格结构兼容性准则），为后续非高斯采样设计提供系统化路径。
尽管安全参数略逊于高斯方案，本工作在物理安全性与工程可行性维度实现了关键跃迁。

AI Summary (English)

We construct preimage sampleable function families (PSFFs) on $q$-ary lattices whose preimage sampling reduces to uniform sampling from simple polytopes—affine transforms of the $\ell_1$ and $\ell_\infty$ balls, and their scaled intersection—over unitriangular lattices. Our samplers adapt and improve the Kannan–Vempala algorithm, requiring only uniform bits and affine linear operations on $[0,1]$-uniform variables—no discrete Gaussians or floating-point arithmetic. Collision resistance relies on the Short Integer Solution (SIS) problem in $\ell_p$ norms. By identifying the Lee metric with the $\ell_1$ norm on $q$-ary lattices, we resolve an open problem posed by Hörmann–van Woerden (CRYPTO’24). We also answer Plançon–Prest’s (PKC’21) question by achieving polytope inradius $\Omega(\sqrt{n}/\!\sqrt{\log n})$, nearly matching the square-root lower bound in lattice rank $n$. We provide a generic framework for PSFFs from polytopes, with explicit geometric sufficiency conditions. While parameters are weaker than Gaussian-based constructions, our scheme offers superior physical security and implementation simplicity.

Abstract

We construct preimage sampleable function families on $q$-ary lattices [Gentry–Peikert–Vaikuntanathan, STOC'08] for which preimage sampling reduces to sampling uniform points of unitriangular lattices from simple polytopes: affine linear transforms on the $\ell_1$ and $\ell_\infty$ balls, and a scaled intersection thereof. We build the necessary samplers by adapting an algorithm of [Kannan–Vempala, STOC'97] and improving its analysis. This sampling requires only uniform bits and affine linear transforms on the uniform distribution on $[0,1]$.
The collision resistance of these families relies on the short integer solutions problem [Ajtai, STOC'96] in various $\ell_p$ norms. Considering the Lee metric as the $\ell_1$ norm in $q$-ary lattices, we answer an open question to construct such families for the Lee metric [Hörmann–van Woerden, CRYPTO'24]. We also answer an open question of [Plançon--Prest, PKC'21] by sampling from polytopes with inradius smaller by a factor almost square root in the lattice rank.
We provide a generic framework for constructing preimage sampleable function families from polytopes with sufficient conditions for realising it. While our parameters are worse than prior discrete Gaussian based constructions, such distributions are challenging from a physical security perspective.

item.title: "Sticking their heads out above the parapets": Lived Experiences of Legal Risks in Research (Extended)
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1207

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景

过度宽泛的计算机犯罪法、知识产权法及其他相关法律（如美国《计算机欺诈与滥用法案》CFAA、英国《计算机滥用法案》CMA）长期构成科研活动的系统性障碍。这些法律未能区分恶意攻击与善意安全研究，致使研究人员在开展公益性质的漏洞挖掘、硬件安全分析或合规数据抓取等关键工作时面临真实且严重的法律风险。尽管学界普遍意识到此类风险，但现有认知多依赖零散个案与传闻，缺乏基于实证的系统性理解。

研究方法

本研究为全球首个聚焦研究人员“亲历体验”的质性研究，通过深度访谈与主题分析，系统考察法律风险对科研实践与研究者身心的实际影响及其应对策略。研究涵盖两类参与者：

  • 36位曾亲身遭遇法律风险的英美研究人员（$N_R=36$），共回溯130项横跨30余年的研究项目与事件；
  • 8位专业支持者（律师、伦理顾问、机构合规官等，$N_S=8$），累计为数千名研究者提供过法律风险应对支持。

主要发现与创新点
